meta.title: Taypro Insights: Solar Cleaning Research for Utility-Scale India
meta.description: Independent research on robotic solar panel cleaning for IPPs and O&M teams, vendor diligence, soiling economics, CAPEX vs Opex, and regional operations in India.

hero.title: Independent research for solar cleaning decisions at MW scale
hero.subtitle: Vendor brochures promise everything. Our reports dig into what actually matters when you specify, contract, and operate robotic cleaning on utility-scale plants in India.

intro.title: Cleaning is an O&M decision, not a line item
intro.p1: At 50–500 MW, how you clean panels affects generation, water use, labour cost, module warranty risk, and whether your O&M contractor can prove they did the work. Most vendor content is sales collateral. Generic blog posts skip the numbers procurement committees need.
intro.p2: Taypro Insights publishes focused research on the questions asset owners and O&M leads actually face, grounded in live industry data, regional soiling context, and the economics of robotic vs manual cleaning in India’s dust belts.

questions.title: Questions our reports help you answer
questions.q1: When does robotic dry cleaning beat manual crews on TCO, and what proof should an RFP require?
questions.q2: How do you evaluate waterless cleaning claims in Rajasthan and Gujarat without risking module coatings?
questions.q3: Should you buy robots (CAPEX), contract managed Opex, or run a hybrid fleet, and who owns uptime risk?
questions.q4: What fleet software and audit trails do you need before accepting a cleaning invoice at 100 MW+?
questions.q5: How often should you clean given local soiling, and when does passive yield loss exceed cleaning spend?
questions.q6: What red flags show up in vendor specs, AMC contracts, and field deployments during technical diligence?

topics.title: Decision areas we research
topics.subtitle: Each edition goes deep on one of these topics. Browse past reports below as they publish.

tools.title: Tools to use alongside this research
tools.calculatorBody: Model CAPEX vs Opex cleaning economics for your plant size and tariff.
tools.compareBody: Side-by-side comparison of labour, water, coverage, and auditability.
tools.methodologyBody: How Taypro defines cleaning performance, use this to evaluate any vendor’s claims.
tools.capexBody: Commercial models for owning robots vs contracting cleaning as a service.

empty.title: First report publishing soon
empty.body: Our inaugural edition on vendor evaluation is in final review. The questions and decision areas above show what each report covers.

pdf.disclaimerShort: Informational procurement research only, not a binding quote or engineering study.
pdf.disclaimerBody: This Taypro Insights report summarizes industry research and Taypro ROI models for utility-scale solar cleaning decisions in India. Figures labelled as industry-typical are directional unless tied to a named source in the report. For site-specific engineering, pricing, or performance guarantees, contact Taypro directly.
pdf.aboutBody: Taypro Private Limited is a Made-in-India manufacturer of autonomous solar panel cleaning robots, headquartered in Chakan, Pune. Taypro's waterless dry-cleaning systems support 5 GW+ of utility and C&I solar capacity across India, helping asset owners recover generation, cut O&M costs, and reduce wet-wash water use. Taypro Insights publishes procurement research grounded in live industry data and deterministic ROI models so IPPs and O&M teams can evaluate cleaning economics and vendor claims with evidence.
pdf.nextStepsBody: Share this report with your O&M or procurement team. Use Taypro's ROI calculator and performance methodology to stress-test vendor claims, or contact us for a site-specific robot count, cycle plan, and quotation.
